tools.name: getNetworks
tools.description: Get all supported blockchain networks. Call this first to discover available networks before using any network-specific function. Returns network IDs like 'ethereum', 'solana', etc. No parameters required.

tools.name: getNetworkDexes
tools.description: Get available DEXes on a specific network. First call getNetworks to see valid network IDs. Required: network. Optional: page, limit, sort_dir (canonical) or sort (legacy alias), sort_by (canonical) or order_by (legacy alias).

tools.name: getNetworkPools
tools.description: Get top liquidity pools on a specific network, sorted by volume by default. The canonical entry point for pool data on one network. Proxies /networks/{network}/pools/search: rows are returned under `results`, with cursor-based pagination. Required: network. Optional: limit, cursor, sort_dir (canonic

tools.name: getDexPools
tools.description: Get pools from a specific DEX on a network. First use getNetworks, then getNetworkDexes to find valid DEX IDs. Required: network, dex. Optional: page, limit, sort_dir (canonical) or sort (legacy alias), sort_by (canonical) or order_by (legacy alias).

tools.name: getNetworkPoolsFilter
tools.description: Filter pools on a network by volume, liquidity, transactions, and creation time. Proxies /networks/{network}/pools/search with filter params; rows are returned under `results`, with cursor-based pagination. REQUIRED: network. OPTIONAL: limit, cursor, volume_24h_min/max, volume_7d_min/max, liquidity_

tools.name: getPoolDetails
tools.description: Get detailed information about a specific pool. Requires network ID from getNetworks and a pool address. Returns comprehensive pool data including tokens, prices, volume, and transaction metrics across multiple timeframes. REQUIRED: network, pool_address. OPTIONAL: inversed.

tools.name: getPoolOHLCV
tools.description: Get historical price data (OHLCV) for a pool - essential for price analysis, backtesting, and visualization. Returns Open-High-Low-Close-Volume data. REQUIRED: network, pool_address, start. OPTIONAL: end, limit, interval, inversed.

tools.name: getPoolTransactions
tools.description: Get recent transactions for a specific pool. Shows swaps, adds, removes with detailed transaction data. Useful for analyzing trading activity and liquidity changes. Use from/to for time-range filtering (UNIX timestamps, results capped to last 7 days). REQUIRED: network, pool_address. OPTIONAL: page,

tools.name: getTokenDetails
tools.description: Get detailed information about a specific token on a network. First use getNetworks to get valid network IDs. Returns token metadata, current price, FDV, liquidity, and trading metrics across multiple timeframes. REQUIRED: network, token_address. No optional parameters.

tools.name: getTokenPools
tools.description: Get liquidity pools containing a specific token on a network. Useful for finding where a token is traded and analyzing its liquidity distribution. Proxies /networks/{network}/pools/search with a token_address filter (the old /tokens/{token_address}/pools endpoint was removed): rows are returned unde

tools.name: getTopTokens
tools.description: Get top tokens on a network ranked by volume, liquidity, transactions, FDV, or 24h price change. Proxies /networks/{network}/tokens/search: rows are returned under `results` (each with address, price_usd, volume_usd_24h, liquidity_usd, fdv_usd, txns_24h, price_change_percentage_24h), with cursor-bas

tools.name: filterNetworkTokens
tools.description: Filter tokens on a network by volume, liquidity, FDV, transactions, and creation time. Proxies /networks/{network}/tokens/search with filter params; rows are returned under `results`, with cursor-based pagination. REQUIRED: network. OPTIONAL: limit, cursor, volume_24h_min/max, liquidity_usd_min/max,

tools.name: getTokenMultiPrices
tools.description: Get batched prices for multiple tokens (max 10 per call) in a single API round-trip. Response shape: { prices: [{id, chain, price_usd}, ...], missing_tokens: [...] }. Tokens that upstream could not price (invalid address, no liquidity, etc.) are surfaced in `missing_tokens` rather than silently drop

tools.name: search
tools.description: Search across ALL networks for tokens, pools, and DEXes by name, symbol, or address. Good starting point when you don't know the specific network. Returns three named arrays: tokens, pools, dexes. NOTE: In `pools[]`, the DEX factory contract address is exposed under `factory_id` (matching `getPoolDe

tools.name: getStats
tools.description: Get high-level statistics about the DexPaprika ecosystem: total networks, DEXes, pools, and tokens available. Provides a quick overview of the platform's coverage. No parameters required.

tools.name: getCapabilities
tools.description: Return server capabilities, workflow patterns, network synonyms, common pitfalls, and best-practice sequences. Use this to onboard agents quickly. No parameters required.

tools.name: submitFeedback
tools.description: Call this when you got stuck, when a tool's response was unexpected, when you needed information that wasn't available, or when something didn't behave as documented. Low friction — submit even partial feedback. We read every submission. Does NOT require a 'rationale' field; the goal/expected/observ

findings.title: Description issues instructions to the model
findings.detail: Description of "getNetworks" contains imperative instructions directed at the AI assistant rather than describing the tool. This is the core tool-poisoning technique.

evidence.snippet: Get all supported blockchain networks. Call this first to discover available networks before using any network-specific function. Returns network IDs like 'ethe…

findings.remediation: Treat the server as untrusted; the model may obey embedded instructions.

findings.title: Description tells the model to hide behavior
findings.detail: Description of "getTokenMultiPrices" instructs the model to conceal actions from the user.

evidence.snippet: Get batched prices for multiple tokens (max 10 per call) in a single API round-trip. Response shape: { prices: [{id, chain, price_usd}, ...], missing_tokens: [.…

findings.title: Generic tool name can shadow trusted tools
findings.detail: Tool "search" uses a generic high-value name. When multiple servers load together, this can intercept calls meant for a trusted server.
